domain: forensic odontology (tooth cementum annulation TCA age estimation)
specific_mechanism: Acellular cementum on tooth roots is deposited in alternating dark/light annual rings (winter/summer). Counting the rings under polarised light microscopy estimates age at death with 85% congruence up to age 30, dropping to 60% over 60. The method has narrow error (1.5 yr to 5 yr) but exhibits a precision-accuracy tradeoff that worsens with age (older rings are compressed and harder to resolve).
llm_application: Forensic 'age' of a trained model: a small null-space probe inserted into each transformer block reads out a count of effective training-data 'rings' encoded in band-pass features of the weight matrix singular values. Bands deposited early in training (wide bands, well-resolved) correspond to high-frequency early-stage data; bands deposited late are compressed and harder to read — analog to TCA's older-age compression. Use the probe-bands count to estimate the model's effective training-step age at audit time, even after fine-tuning has obscured the absolute step counter.
novelty_claim: Probing hidden states for hidden information (medrxiv 2025.09.17), training-dynamics analyses, and quantization-band literature catalogue parameter-spectrum properties but do not propose a null-space probe that reads model age from SVD-band annulation as a forensic technique. Open question whether this is substantively new or implicit in singular-value-spectrum analyses.
